prediction_ref.short_label: Frontier AI lab × Tier-1 cloud capacity capture splits the field cleanly by Q3 2026; AWS / Azure / GCP form the three-cloud training oligopoly while Oracle / IBM / Coreweave shift to inference-only

validation_rows.evidence_summary: May 1's Pentagon 7-vendor classified-network AI deal (AWS, Microsoft, Google, NVIDIA, OpenAI, SpaceX, Reflection — Anthropic excluded as supply-chain risk) reinforces the AWS/Azure/GCP capacity-capture clustering, but BofA's $1.3T 2026 chips forecast and Apple's record-day tape do not directly speak to Oracle/IBM/Coreweave's training-vs-inference split.

bridge.narrative: Today's Pentagon 7-vendor classified-network deal cements AWS, Microsoft, and Google inside the federally-sanctioned frontier-training cohort while leaving Oracle and Coreweave conspicuously absent — a structural-force tap on the predicted three-cloud training oligopoly without yet showing the inference-only pivot.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit Oracle, IBM, or Coreweave roadmap or earnings line that names training-pullback or inference-specialization.

bridge.remaining_gap: No OEM has yet published a continuous-operation-hours or MTBF figure for an Apptronik deployment that league tables could index.

prediction_ref.short_label: MCP-protocol attack surface becomes biggest enterprise AI adoption bottleneck by Q3 2026; OWASP MCP Top 10 + MCP-over-mTLS + MCP Gateway / Firewall products roll out

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Microsoft Agent 365 GA May 1 ships tenant-level MCP-server allow/block as the first mainstream-priced enterprise gate; CSA's Apr 29 CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA milestones authorize the agentic-AI CVE issuance lane that an OWASP MCP Top 10 will draw from; ATT&CK v19 already names MCP server compromise as a technique; CSA's May 1 zero-trust-for-AI piece reframes identity for the agent fleet.

bridge.remaining_gap: Still missing a publicly drafted OWASP MCP Top 10 working-group call or a posted MCP-over-mTLS RFC from Anthropic, Microsoft, Google, Cisco, or Cloudflare.

prediction_ref.short_label: Hyperscaler-AI-lab capital coupling graduates from finance pact to compute-stack lock-in by Q3 2026; frontier-model serving topology dictated by funding origin; multi-frontier-vendor replaces multi-runtime

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Pentagon May 1 explicitly excludes Anthropic from the 7-vendor classified-network deal as a supply-chain risk while AWS retains Trainium-coupled Anthropic deployment; AWS Bedrock now hosts OpenAI models and Codex (limited preview); Anthropic Opus 4.7 ships natively through the Trainium / TPU paths announced in April.

bridge.remaining_gap: No DeepSeek V4 or Qwen 3.6 deployment announcement on Huawei Ascend has landed in this news window to confirm the symmetric Chinese-lab compute lock.

prediction_ref.short_label: Inference servers become new "primitive supply chain" — Q3 2026 sees first AI-Infra-specific CVE class with mandatory NVD tagging

validation_rows.evidence_summary: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) remains unpatched ~13 days post-disclosure into May 2; LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQLi exploited within 26 hours leaks OpenAI/Anthropic/Bedrock keys; UTT HiPER 1200GW CVE-2026-7512 + IBM Langflow CVE-2026-6543 disclosed May 1; CISA's May 1 KEV adds CVE-2026-31431 Linux Copy Fail.

bridge.remaining_gap: NVD has not yet posted an explicit AI-Infra schema field or working-group note announcing one.
